title: Odontoloma bifidum
taxonomy: Odontoloma bifidum is a species of fungus beetle in the family Mordellidae. It belongs to the kingdom Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, class Insecta, order Coleoptera, and family Mordellidae. Within the family Mordellidae, it is part of the subfamily Mordellinae. Odontoloma bifidum is a type of fungus-gnating beetle, known for its distinctive habits of living on fungi and its elongated body shape. This beetle's classification is based on its physical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other members of the family Mordellidae.
uses: Odontoloma bifidum, a beetle species, does not have known medicinal uses. However, its beetle larvae are often associated with fungi and plants that have potential medicinal properties. The fungi and plants associated with Odontoloma bifidum, such as certain species of fungi in the genus Cortinarius, have been studied for their psychoactive and medicinal properties. intro: Odontoloma bifidum, also known as Toothache Plant, is a tropical plant species that belongs to the Acanthaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to alleviate toothache and other oral health issues due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for soothing mouth ulcers and gum inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Odontoloma bifidum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Odontoloma bifidum is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, making it a valuable remedy for oral health care.
